description: Browser Privacy Monitor Phase 3 - Sensitive Field Protection Pipeline
_readme: Wazuh uses Filebeat to forward alerts from the Manager to the Indexer.
Filebeat already runs its own pipeline called:
The pipeline file that controls this lives on the WAZUH MANAGER at:
2. Add the processors from this file into Filebeat's pipeline.json,
inside the existing 'processors' array — before the final remove blocks.
3. Reload the pipeline into the Indexer:
OPTIONAL METHOD (Method B — Separate pipeline via API):
Only needed if you want a completely separate pipeline as extra hardening.
DO NOT apply as default_pipeline to wazuh-alerts-4.x-* — it will conflict.
c={k:v for k,v in d.items() if not k.startswith('_')}
c['processors']=[{k2:v2 for k2,v2 in p.items() if k2!='_comment'} for p in c['processors']]
NOTE: Layer 1 (endpoint redaction) already protects 99% of sensitive values.
This pipeline is optional Layer 2 hardening — a safety net only.

processors._comment: Strip URL fragment — fragments can silently carry tokens (e.g. #access_token=...)
Catch any high-entropy token (64+ chars) that slipped past endpoint redaction in url_redacted

script.source: if (ctx.data != null && ctx.data.url_redacted != null) { def url = ctx.data.url_redacted; def tokenPattern = /[A-Za-z0-9+\/_~\-]{64,}/; if (tokenPattern.matcher(url).find()) { ctx.data.url_redacted = '[PIPELINE-MASKED: high-entropy value detected]'; ctx.data.pipeline_masked = true; } }

processors._comment: Hard-drop any raw URL fields if they accidentally appear — should never happen after Layer 1
Hard-drop any raw title fields if they accidentally appear
Normalise risk_score to integer so Wazuh Dashboard range filters work correctly
Normalise sensitive_detected to boolean for dashboard filter accuracy
Audit flag — marks documents that passed through this pipeline
